crime.value: Apparent suicide pact of three people found dead in a Lexus at a Shinto shrine
crime.quote: it looked like a garden-variety suicide pact, the kind that had become all too common

victim.value: Three people: middle-aged couple and young Goth-Lolita woman with unclear connection to the couple
victim.quote: A middle-aged man and woman in front, a young woman in back

closed_world.value: Tokyo Metropolitan Police jurisdiction, specifically the Shinto shrine location
closed_world.quote: one of them had noticed the lone Lexus shortly after 7:30 a.m. and had called the police

investigator.value: Tokyo Metropolitan Police Detective Kenji Nakamura, nearly one year on the job
investigator.quote: Kenji had been a detective for nearly a year, but these were the first suspicious deaths

red_herring.value: Apparent suicide pact setup designed to misdirect from murder investigation
red_herring.quote: it looked like a garden-variety suicide pact, the kind that had become all too common

clue_fairness.value: Blank suicide note and missing ID presented to reader as detective discovers them

social_world.value: Tokyo police hierarchy and Japanese cultural expectations around crime and confession
social_world.quote: murder was rare, usually the work of drunken family members who dutifully turned themselves in

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical evidence contradicting suicide narrative - blank note, missing identification

media_and_audience.value: Internet-facilitated suicide pacts as cultural phenomenon shaping initial interpretation
media_and_audience.quote: Now that the Internet made it so convenient, the despairing could plan their final deadly get-togethers

investigator_wound.value: Inexperienced detective facing first suspicious deaths case
investigator_wound.quote: Kenji had been a detective for nearly a year, but these were the first suspicious deaths

_meta.title: ebook jonelle patrick nightshade an only in tokyo mystery
